docs.title: DictaPilot3 Documentation
docs.text: Welcome to DictaPilot3 - cross-platform voice dictation with smart editing.

docs.title: What is DictaPilot3?
docs.text: DictaPilot3 is an open-source voice dictation tool that lets you transcribe speech to text using a simple hold-to-talk workflow. Hold a hotkey (F9 by default), speak, release, and your words are transcribed and pasted into any application.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Hold-to-Talk - Press F9, speak, release. That's it.
  • Smart Voice Commands - "delete that", "replace X with Y", "rewrite formal"
  • Delta Paste - Only sends changed characters, not full text (faster, less flicker)
  • Context-Aware - Different settings per application
  • Developer-Focused - Agent mode for coding workflows
  • Privacy-First - Local storage, open source

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Hold F9] --> B[Speak]
    B --> C[Release F9]
    C --> D[Transcription]
    D --> E[Smart Editing]
    E --> F[Auto-Paste]
    F --> G[Text Appears]
  1. Hold F9 - Start recording
  2. Speak naturally - Audio captured
  3. Release F9 - Transcription begins
  4. Smart editing - Commands processed, text cleaned
  5. Auto-paste - Text appears in your app

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/RehanSajid136602/DictaPilot.git
cd DictaPilot

# Run setup script
./setup/setup_linux.sh      # Linux
./setup/setup_macos.command  # macOS
setup\setup_windows.bat      # Windows

# Add API key
echo "GROQ_API_KEY=your_key_here" > .env

# Start dictating
python app.py

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
Basic dictation: 

You say: "Hello world"
Result: "Hello world"

With voice commands: 

You say: "Hello world... delete that... Goodbye world"
Result: "Goodbye world"

Smart editing: 

You say: "um this is uh a test"
Result: "This is a test."

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text: Common questions about DictaPilot3.

docs.title: What is DictaPilot3?
docs.text: DictaPilot3 is a cross-platform voice dictation tool that lets you transcribe speech to text using a simple hold-to-talk workflow. Hold a hotkey (F9 by default), speak, release, and your words are transcribed and pasted into any application.

docs.title: How is it different from other dictation tools?
docs.text:
• Open source - Free and transparent
  • Smart editing - Voice commands like "delete that", "replace X with Y"
  • Delta paste - Only sends changed characters, not full text
  • Context-aware - Different settings per application
  • Developer-focused - Agent mode for coding workflows
  • Privacy-first - Local storage, optional cloud

docs.title: Does it work offline?
docs.text: No, DictaPilot3 requires internet connection for the Groq API. However, you can use local whisper.cpp as an alternative backend for offline transcription (requires additional setup).

docs.title: Is it free?
docs.text: Yes, DictaPilot3 is free and open source (MIT license). You need a free Groq API key which has generous usage limits.

docs.title: What platforms are supported?
docs.text:
• Linux (X11 and Wayland)
  • macOS (10.14+)
  • Windows (10/11)

docs.title: How do I get a Groq API key?
docs.text:
1. Visit console.groq.com
  2. Sign up or log in (free)
  3. Create a new API key
  4. Copy the key (starts with gsk_)
  5. Add to .env file: GROQ_API_KEY=your_key_here

docs.title: Can I change the hotkey?
docs.text:
Yes, edit .env file:

HOTKEY=f10           # Single key
HOTKEY=ctrl+shift+d  # Key combination
HOTKEY=alt+space     # Alternative

docs.title: How do I select a different microphone?
docs.text:
List available devices: 

python -c "import sounddevice; print(sounddevice.query_devices())"

Set device in .env: 

AUDIO_DEVICE=1  # Use index from list

docs.title: Can I use it with multiple languages?
docs.text: Yes, Whisper supports 100+ languages. The model auto-detects language, or you can set it per profile.

docs.title: Where is my data stored?
docs.text:
• Transcriptions: Stored locally in ~/.local/share/dictapilot/transcriptions.json (Linux/macOS) or %APPDATA%\DictaPilot\transcriptions.json (Windows)
  • Audio: Not stored (deleted after transcription)
  • API key: Stored in .env file or system keychain (macOS)

docs.title: Is my audio sent to the cloud?
docs.text: Yes, audio is sent to Groq API for transcription. Groq's privacy policy applies. For offline transcription, use local whisper.cpp backend.

docs.title: Can I use it for sensitive information?
docs.text:
• Audio is sent to Groq API (cloud processing)
  • Transcriptions stored locally
  • For maximum privacy, use local whisper.cpp backend
  • Don't use for passwords or highly sensitive data

docs.title: Does it collect telemetry?
docs.text: No, DictaPilot3 does not collect any telemetry or usage data.

docs.title: What voice commands are supported?
docs.text:
Common commands: - "delete that" - Remove last segment - "clear all" - Clear everything - "replace X with Y" - Replace text - "ignore" - Don't include this - "rewrite formal" - Change tone - "fix grammar" - Correct grammar

See Voice Commands Reference for complete list.

docs.title: What is delta paste?
docs.text:
Delta paste only sends the characters that changed, not the entire text. This is faster and reduces flicker compared to traditional paste methods.

Example: - Old text: "Hello world" - New text: "Hello there world" - Delta paste: Only sends "there " (not the full text)

docs.title: What is agent mode?
docs.text:
Agent mode formats your dictation as structured prompts for coding assistants. It extracts: - Task description - File paths and code locations - Constraints and requirements - Acceptance criteria

Useful for dictating coding tasks to AI assistants.

docs.title: What is smart editing?
docs.text:
Smart editing automatically: - Removes filler words (um, uh, etc.) - Fixes repeated words - Handles inline corrections ("no, I mean...") - Processes voice commands - Cleans up punctuation

Can use heuristic rules (fast) or LLM (more accurate).

docs.title: What are context-aware profiles?
docs.text: Profiles let you configure different settings per application: - Tone (formal, casual, etc.) - Language - Custom dictionary - Cleanup level

docs.title: How do I customize the floating window appearance?
docs.text:
Edit .env file to customize the modern UI:

FLOATING_UI_STYLE=modern          # or "classic" for old design
FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=blue        # blue, purple, or green
FLOATING_GLASSMORPHISM=1          # enable glass effect
FLOATING_ANIMATIONS=1             # enable animations
FLOATING_REDUCED_MOTION=0         # accessibility option

See Modern UI Guide for detailed customization options.

docs.title: Can I disable animations?
docs.text:
Yes, there are two ways:

  1. Disable all animations: 

    FLOATING_ANIMATIONS=0

  2. Enable reduced motion (accessibility): 

    FLOATING_REDUCED_MOTION=1

Reduced motion keeps essential animations but removes decorative effects.

docs.title: What are the accent color options?
docs.text:
Three accent colors are available:

  • Blue (default) - Professional and trustworthy (#3B82F6)
  • Purple - Creative and modern (#8B5CF6)
  • Green - Positive and energetic (#10B981)

Set in .env: 

FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=purple

docs.title: How do I switch back to the classic UI?
docs.text:
Set FLOATING_UI_STYLE=classic in .env:

FLOATING_UI_STYLE=classic

Restart DictaPilot for changes to take effect. Classic UI uses the original design without glassmorphism or modern animations.

docs.title: Why are animations choppy?
docs.text:
Choppy animations can be caused by:

  1. High CPU usage - Close other applications
  2. Low-end hardware - Enable reduced motion or disable animations
  3. Compositor issues (Linux) - Check if compositor is running

Solutions: 

FLOATING_REDUCED_MOTION=1    # Reduce animation complexity
FLOATING_ANIMATIONS=0        # Disable animations completely
FLOATING_UI_STYLE=classic    # Use classic UI

docs.title: Does the modern UI work on all platforms?
docs.text:
Yes, the modern UI works on: - Linux (X11 and Wayland with compositor) - macOS (10.14+) - Windows (10/11)

Note: Glassmorphism requires compositor support: - Linux: Enable compositor (usually enabled by default on modern desktops) - macOS: Works out of the box - Windows: Works on Windows 10/11

If transparency doesn't work, the UI will fall back gracefully or you can use classic mode.

docs.title: Can I change the floating window size?
docs.text:
Yes, set dimensions in .env:

FLOATING_WIDTH=200
FLOATING_HEIGHT=50

The window will scale appropriately while maintaining the modern design.

Example: Use formal tone in email, casual in chat apps.

docs.title: Why isn't my hotkey working?
docs.text:
Common causes: 1. Backend issue - Try different HOTKEY_BACKEND 2. Conflict - Another app using same hotkey 3. Permissions - macOS needs Accessibility permission 4. Wrong key format - Check syntax in .env

See Troubleshooting Guide for detailed solutions.

docs.title: Why isn't text pasting?
docs.text:
Common causes: 1. Backend issue - Try different PASTE_BACKEND 2. Permissions - macOS needs Accessibility permission 3. App blocks input - Some apps don't accept programmatic paste 4. Focus issue - Text field not focused

See Troubleshooting Guide for detailed solutions.

docs.title: Why isn't it working on Wayland?
docs.text:
DictaPilot3 now has native Wayland support! If you have issues:

  1. Install Wayland dependencies: 

    sudo apt install wl-clipboard wtype  # Ubuntu/Debian
sudo dnf install wl-clipboard wtype  # Fedora
sudo pacman -S wl-clipboard wtype    # Arch

  2. Check dependencies: 

    python app.py --wayland-deps

  3. Use Wayland backends: 

    HOTKEY_BACKEND=wayland
PASTE_BACKEND=wayland

  4. Fallback options: 

    HOTKEY_BACKEND=pynput
PASTE_BACKEND=xdotool

See Linux Guide for detailed Wayland setup.

docs.title: Why is transcription quality poor?
docs.text: Improve quality: - Use external microphone - Reduce background noise - Speak clearly at normal pace - Use DICTATION_MODE=accurate - Try GROQ_WHISPER_MODEL=whisper-large-v3

docs.title: Why is it slow?
docs.text: Speed it up: - Use GROQ_WHISPER_MODEL=whisper-large-v3-turbo - Set INSTANT_REFINE=0 - Use SMART_MODE=heuristic - Use PASTE_MODE=delta

docs.title: How do I dictate punctuation?
docs.text: Just speak naturally - Whisper adds punctuation automatically. You can also say: - "period" or "full stop" - "comma" - "question mark" - "exclamation point"

docs.title: Can I dictate code?
docs.text: Yes! Tips for code dictation: - Speak clearly: "def hello world function" - Use agent mode for structured tasks - Create custom snippets for common patterns - Use voice commands to edit

docs.title: How do I undo mistakes?
docs.text: Say "delete that" or "undo" to remove the last segment. Or use "clear all" to start over.

docs.title: Can I use it while typing?
docs.text: Yes, but it's designed for pure dictation. Mixing typing and dictation works but may be less efficient.

docs.title: Does it work in all applications?
docs.text: Works in most applications that accept text input. Some exceptions: - Password fields (security restriction) - Some games (may not accept programmatic input) - Virtual machines (may need special configuration)

docs.title: Can I customize voice commands?
docs.text: Currently, voice commands are built-in. Custom commands are planned for future releases.

docs.title: Can I add custom words to dictionary?
docs.text:
Yes, create dictionary.json in config directory:

{
  "adelant": "Adelant",
  "dictapilot": "DictaPilot"
}

docs.title: How do I create custom profiles?
docs.text: Create profile_bundle.json in config directory. See Profile Ingestion Spec for format.

docs.title: Can I use local Whisper instead of Groq?
docs.text:
Yes, but requires additional setup: 1. Install whisper.cpp 2. Download GGML model 3. Configure WHISPER_BACKEND=local

(Detailed guide coming soon)

docs.title: Can I contribute?
docs.text: Yes! DictaPilot3 is open source. See Contributing Guide for details.

docs.title: How does it compare to Dragon NaturallySpeaking?
docs.text:
DictaPilot3: - Free and open source - Cloud-based (requires internet) - Modern AI models (Whisper) - Cross-platform - Developer-focused features

Dragon: - Commercial ($150-300) - Offline capable - More mature - Better for medical/legal - Windows/macOS only

docs.title: How does it compare to built-in OS dictation?
docs.text:
DictaPilot3: - Smart editing and voice commands - Context-aware profiles - Delta paste technology - Customizable - Works across all apps

OS Dictation: - Built-in (no setup) - Basic transcription only - Limited customization - May have better OS integration

docs.title: How does it compare to WhisperFlow?
docs.text:
DictaPilot3: - Open source - Free (API costs only) - Developer-focused - Terminal-first - Extensive customization

WhisperFlow: - Commercial ($12/month) - Mobile apps - Polished GUI - Real-time streaming - Enterprise features

See Comparison Document for detailed analysis.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: DictaPilot's floating window has been redesigned with modern 2026 UI/UX principles, featuring glassmorphism, smooth animations, and intuitive visual feedback.

docs.title: Glassmorphism (Liquid Glass)
docs.text:
• Frosted glass effect with translucent background
  • Backdrop blur for depth and layering
  • Subtle borders with semi-transparent edges
  • Vibrant accent colors with proper contrast

docs.title: Smooth Animations
docs.text:
• Breathing animation in idle state
  • Pulsing glow during active recording
  • Smooth state transitions with elastic easing
  • Hover effects with subtle scale changes

docs.title: Modern Waveform Visualization
docs.text:
• 7 elegant bars with smooth interpolation
  • Gradient fills (light → primary → dark)
  • Glow effects during active states
  • Responsive to audio input levels

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Add these settings to your .env file:

# UI Style (modern or classic)
FLOATING_UI_STYLE=modern

# Accent Color (blue, purple, green)
FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=blue

# Enable glassmorphism effect
FLOATING_GLASSMORPHISM=1

# Enable animations
FLOATING_ANIMATIONS=1

# Reduced motion for accessibility
FLOATING_REDUCED_MOTION=0

# Layout style (circular, pill, card)
FLOATING_LAYOUT=pill

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Define a stable contract for collecting profile information on a website and importing it into DictaPilot with high consistency and low risk.

docs.title: Transport Model
docs.text:
• Website exports a JSON bundle.
  • App imports bundle via local file placement or explicit import flow.
  • Default local path: ~/.config/dictapilot/profile_bundle.json (or %APPDATA%/DictaPilot/profile_bundle.json on Windows via existing config dir logic).
  • Override path supported with PROFILE_BUNDLE_PATH.

docs.title: Bundle Schema (v1)
docs.text:
{
  "version": 1,
  "default_profile": "default",
  "source_url": "https://your-site.example/profiles/abc",
  "profiles": [
    {
      "id": "default",
      "name": "Default",
      "tone": "polite",
      "language": "english",
      "role": "general assistant",
      "domain": "general",
      "glossary": {
        "SLA": "service level agreement"
      }
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Validation Rules
docs.text:
• profiles must contain at least one profile.
  • Profile id is required (case-insensitive, normalized to lowercase in app).
  • glossary must be an object of non-empty string key/value pairs.
  • Unknown fields are ignored (forward compatibility).

docs.title: Runtime Resolution
docs.text:
1. App loads legacy app profiles (profiles.json) for per-app tone/language overrides.
  2. App loads website profile bundle (profile_bundle.json).
  3. Active profile is selected from:
  4. ACTIVE_PROFILE env var, else
  5. default_profile in bundle, else
  6. default.
  7. Final context merge order:
  8. Legacy defaults → Active profile → Per-app overrides.

docs.title: Security/Privacy
docs.text:
• No remote fetch in runtime path by default.
  • Bundle import is local-first.
  • Avoid storing secrets in profile bundle.
  • Keep profile data limited to dictation behavior fields only.

docs.title: Observability (recommended next)
docs.text:
• Track active profile ID in debug logs.
  • Track ingestion validation errors with actionable messages.
  • Track profile usage counters locally.

docs.title: Quick Start Guide
docs.text: Get DictaPilot3 up and running in under 5 minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.10 or higher
  • Microphone
  • Groq API key (free at console.groq.com)

docs.title: Step 1: Install Dependencies
docs.text:
Clone the repository and install dependencies:

git clone https://github.com/RehanSajid136602/DictaPilot.git
cd DictaPilot

Run the setup script for your platform:

Linux: 

./setup/setup_linux.sh

macOS: 

./setup/setup_macos.command

Windows: 

setup\setup_windows.bat

Verification: Check that Python packages are installed: 

python -c "import groq, sounddevice, PySide6; print('Dependencies OK')"

docs.title: Step 2: Configure API Key
docs.text:
Get your free Groq API key from console.groq.com.

Create a .env file in the project root:

echo "GROQ_API_KEY=your_api_key_here" > .env

Verification: Test API connectivity: 

python -c "from groq import Groq; import os; from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv(); client = Groq(api_key=os.getenv('GROQ_API_KEY')); print('API key valid')"

docs.title: Step 3: Run DictaPilot
docs.text:
Start the application:

python app.py

You should see: - "DictaPilot started" message - Floating status window (optional) - System tray icon (if available)

Verification: Check that the app is running without errors.

docs.title: Step 4: Configure Hotkey (Optional)
docs.text:
The default hotkey is F9. To change it, edit .env:

HOTKEY=f10

Available keys: f1-f12, ctrl+shift+d, etc.

Verification: Press your hotkey - the floating window should show "Recording" state.

docs.title: Step 5: Test Dictation
docs.text:
1. Open any text editor (Notepad, VS Code, etc.)
  2. Click in a text field
  3. Hold the hotkey (F9)
  4. Speak: "Hello world, this is a test"
  5. Release the hotkey
  6. Text should appear in your editor

Verification: If text appears correctly, setup is complete! 🎉

docs.title: Step 6: Customize Modern UI (Optional)
docs.text:
The floating window uses a modern glassmorphism design by default. Customize in .env:

FLOATING_UI_STYLE=modern          # or "classic" for old design
FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=blue        # blue, purple, or green
FLOATING_GLASSMORPHISM=1          # enable glass effect
FLOATING_ANIMATIONS=1             # enable animations
FLOATING_REDUCED_MOTION=0         # accessibility option

Modern UI Features: - Translucent glass effect with backdrop blur - Smooth 60 FPS animations (breathing, pulsing) - Three accent color themes - Reduced motion support for accessibility

See full guide: Modern UI Guide

Verification: Change FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=purple and restart to see the purple theme.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Hotkey not working? - Linux: See Linux Platform Guide for X11/Wayland setup - macOS: Grant Accessibility permissions in System Preferences - Windows: Run as administrator if needed

Text not pasting? - Check that you released the hotkey - Try different paste backend: PASTE_BACKEND=xdotool (Linux) or PASTE_BACKEND=osascript (macOS)

API errors? - Verify API key is correct in .env - Check internet connection - See Troubleshooting Guide

Audio issues? - Check microphone permissions - List available devices: python -c "import sounddevice; print(sounddevice.query_devices())" - Set specific device: AUDIO_DEVICE=1 in .env

Animation performance issues? - Enable reduced motion: FLOATING_REDUCED_MOTION=1 - Disable animations: FLOATING_ANIMATIONS=0 - Switch to classic UI: FLOATING_UI_STYLE=classic

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: DictaPilot's Spec Mode transforms voice dictation into structured specifications that can be sent directly to IDE agents and AI coding assistants. This enables a voice-first, specification-driven workflow inspired by Luna Drive and modern agentic coding patterns.

docs.title: 1. Enable Spec Mode
docs.text:
Add to your .env file: 

SPEC_MODE_ENABLED=1
SPEC_AUTO_DETECT_INTENT=1
SPEC_TEMPLATE=standard

docs.title: 2. Start a New Spec
docs.text:
Press your hotkey (F9) and say: 

"Start new spec: Add dark mode to settings dashboard"

docs.title: 3. Add Details
docs.text:
Continue speaking to add context and criteria: 

"Context: Users need dark mode for late-night coding sessions. 
Acceptance criteria: Toggle in settings, persists across sessions, applies to all views.
Constraint: Must support system theme detection."

docs.title: 4. Send to IDE
docs.text: "Send to Cursor"

docs.title: Creating Specs
docs.text:
• "Start new spec: [title]" - Create a new specification
  • "Create feature spec: [title]" - Create a feature specification
  • "Create bugfix spec: [title]" - Create a bug fix specification
  • "Create refactor spec: [title]" - Create a refactoring specification

docs.title: Adding Content
docs.text:
• "Goal: [description]" - Set the goal/objective
  • "Context: [description]" - Add background context
  • "Acceptance criteria: [criterion]" - Add acceptance criterion
  • "Constraint: [constraint]" - Add a constraint
  • "File: [path]" - Reference a file location
  • "Non-goal: [description]" - Add a non-goal

docs.title: Managing Specs
docs.text:
• "Save spec" - Save current specification
  • "Export as [format]" - Export spec (formats: standard, openspec, luna, github)
  • "Send to [agent]" - Send to IDE agent (cursor, windsurf, cline, luna)
  • "List specs" - List saved specifications
  • "Load spec: [title]" - Load a saved specification

docs.title: Standard Template
docs.text: General-purpose specification with all sections.

docs.title: Feature Template
docs.text: Optimized for new feature development: - Goal - Context - Acceptance Criteria - Constraints - Files/Locations

docs.title: Bugfix Template
docs.text: Optimized for bug fixes: - Bug Description - Steps to Reproduce - Expected Behavior - Acceptance Criteria

docs.title: Refactor Template
docs.text: Optimized for refactoring: - Current State - Target State - Acceptance Criteria - Non-Goals (what not to change)

docs.title: Minimal Template
docs.text: Quick specs with just goal and acceptance criteria.

docs.title: Standard Format
docs.text:
# Feature Title

## Goal
[Description]

## Context
[Background]

## Acceptance Criteria
- [Criterion 1]
- [Criterion 2]

## Constraints
- [Constraint 1]

docs.title: OpenSpec Format
docs.text:
Compatible with OpenSpec workflow tools: 

# Feature Title

**Created:** 2026-02-18T16:55:00Z
**Updated:** 2026-02-18T16:55:00Z

## Overview
[Description]

## Requirements
1. [Requirement 1]
2. [Requirement 2]

docs.title: Luna Format
docs.text:
Compatible with Luna Drive: 

# Specification: Feature Title

## Intent
[Description]

## Success Criteria
- [ ] [Criterion 1]
- [ ] [Criterion 2]

## Boundaries
- [Constraint 1]

docs.title: GitHub Format
docs.text:
Compatible with GitHub issues/PRs: 

## Feature Title

### Description
[Description]

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] [Criterion 1]
- [ ] [Criterion 2]

docs.title: Cursor
docs.text: Specs are saved to ~/.cursor/dictapilot_input.md and Cursor is triggered to open them.

docs.title: Windsurf
docs.text: Specs are saved to ~/.windsurf/dictapilot_input.md.

docs.title: Cline
docs.text: Specs are saved to ~/.vscode/cline/dictapilot_input.md.

docs.title: Luna Drive
docs.text: Specs are sent via API to Luna Drive endpoint (requires API key).

docs.title: Custom Webhooks
docs.text:
Configure custom endpoints in AGENT_ENDPOINTS: 

AGENT_ENDPOINTS='{"myagent": "https://api.example.com/spec"}'

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
# Enable spec mode
SPEC_MODE_ENABLED=1

# Default template (standard, minimal, detailed, openspec, luna, github)
SPEC_TEMPLATE=standard

# Auto-detect intent (spec vs code vs docs)
SPEC_AUTO_DETECT_INTENT=1

# Default export format
SPEC_FORMAT=standard

# Enable spec versioning
SPEC_STORAGE_ENABLED=1

# Agent endpoints (JSON)
AGENT_ENDPOINTS='{"cursor": "http://localhost:9333"}'

# Workflow format
WORKFLOW_FORMAT=openspec

docs.title: Dashboard Configuration
docs.text: Access spec mode settings in the Agent View of the dashboard: - Enable/disable spec mode - Select default template - Configure agent connections - View spec history

docs.title: Example 1: Voice-Driven Feature Development
docs.text:
1. Start Spec 

    "Start new spec: Add user authentication"

  2. Add Details 

    "Goal: Implement secure user authentication with JWT tokens.
Context: Currently no authentication, need to protect API endpoints.
Acceptance criteria: Users can register, login, logout, JWT tokens expire after 24 hours.
Constraint: Must use bcrypt for password hashing.
File: app.py, auth.py"

  3. Save and Send 

    "Save spec. Send to Cursor."

docs.title: Example 2: Quick Bug Fix
docs.text:
1. Start Bugfix Spec 

    "Create bugfix spec: Fix login redirect loop"

  2. Describe Bug 

    "Context: Users get stuck in redirect loop after login.
Acceptance criteria: Login redirects to dashboard once, no loops."

  3. Send to Agent 

    "Send to Windsurf"

docs.title: Example 3: Refactoring
docs.text:
1. Start Refactor Spec 

    "Create refactor spec: Extract database logic to repository pattern"

  2. Add Details 

    "Goal: Separate database logic from business logic.
Context: Current code has SQL queries mixed with business logic.
Acceptance criteria: All database access through repository classes, tests pass.
Non-goal: Don't change the API interface."

  3. Export for Review 

    "Export as GitHub format"

docs.title: Intent Detection
docs.text: Spec mode automatically detects your intent:

docs.title: Spec Intent
docs.text: Triggered by keywords: "spec", "requirement", "goal", "acceptance criteria", "we need to"

docs.title: Code Intent
docs.text: Triggered by keywords: "function", "class", "method", "implement", "write code"

docs.title: Documentation Intent
docs.text: Triggered by keywords: "document", "readme", "guide", "comment", "docstring"

docs.title: Review Intent
docs.text: Triggered by keywords: "review", "feedback", "issue", "bug", "refactor"

docs.title: Storage and Versioning
docs.text:
Specs are stored in: - Linux/macOS: ~/.local/share/dictapilot/specs/ - Windows: %APPDATA%\DictaPilot\specs\

Each spec includes: - spec.md - Markdown version - spec.json - JSON version for parsing - versions/ - Version history

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Be Specific: Clear goals and acceptance criteria lead to better implementations
  2. Use Templates: Choose the right template for your task
  3. Add Context: Background information helps agents understand the problem
  4. Define Constraints: Specify what must/must not be done
  5. Reference Files: Mention specific files that need changes
  6. Version Specs: Save specs before major changes
  7. Review Before Sending: Export and review before sending to agents

docs.title: Spec Mode Not Activating
docs.text:
• Check SPEC_MODE_ENABLED=1 in .env
  • Verify SPEC_AUTO_DETECT_INTENT=1
  • Use explicit trigger: "Start new spec"

docs.title: Agent Not Receiving Specs
docs.text:
• Check agent connection in dashboard
  • Verify agent endpoint configuration
  • Test connection with "Test connection to [agent]"

docs.title: Intent Misclassified
docs.text:
• Use explicit commands: "Start new spec", "Write code", etc.
  • Adjust SPEC_AUTO_DETECT_INTENT if needed
  • Check intent confidence in dashboard

docs.title: Custom Templates
docs.text: Create custom templates by extending SpecTemplate class in spec_generator.py.

docs.title: Custom Agents
docs.text: Add custom agents in agent_orchestrator.py or via webhook configuration.

docs.title: Workflow Automation
docs.text: Use workflow_engine.py to create custom workflows that chain spec creation, agent handoff, and implementation.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: See spec_generator.py, intent_classifier.py, spec_store.py, agent_orchestrator.py, and workflow_engine.py for detailed API documentation.

docs.title: Spec Mode Quick Start
docs.text: Transform your voice into structured specifications that AI agents can implement.

docs.title: 1. Enable Spec Mode
docs.text:
Add to .env: 

SPEC_MODE_ENABLED=1
SPEC_AUTO_DETECT_INTENT=1

docs.title: 2. Try It Out
docs.text:
Press F9 and say: 

"Start new spec: Add dark mode toggle to settings"

Continue: 

"Context: Users want dark mode for night coding.
Acceptance criteria: Toggle button in settings, persists on reload, applies to all views.
Constraint: Must support system theme detection."

docs.title: 3. Send to Your IDE
docs.text:
"Send to Cursor"

Done! Your spec is now in Cursor ready for implementation.

docs.title: Voice Commands Cheat Sheet
docs.text: Say This Result "Start new spec: [title]" Create new specification "Goal: [description]" Set the goal "Context: [info]" Add background "Acceptance criteria: [criterion]" Add success criterion "Constraint: [limit]" Add constraint "Save spec" Save to storage "Send to Cursor" Send to Cursor IDE "Export as Luna" Export Luna format

docs.text:

  • openspec - OpenSpec workflow format

docs.title: Example Workflow
docs.text:
You: "Start new spec: User authentication with JWT"

You: "Goal: Implement secure authentication.
      Context: No auth currently, need to protect API.
      Acceptance criteria: Register, login, logout, JWT expires in 24h.
      Constraint: Use bcrypt for passwords.

You: "Save spec. Send to Cursor."

✓ Spec saved and sent to Cursor!

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Spec mode not working? - Check SPEC_MODE_ENABLED=1 in .env - Use explicit trigger: "Start new spec"

Agent not receiving specs? - Verify agent is running - Check dashboard Agent View for connection status

Need help? - See full documentation - Check FAQ

docs.title: Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text: Common issues and solutions for DictaPilot3.

docs.title: Quick Diagnostics
docs.text:
Run these commands to check system status:

# Check Python and dependencies
python --version
python -c "import groq, sounddevice, PySide6; print('Dependencies OK')"

# Check API connectivity
python -c "from groq import Groq; import os; from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv(); client = Groq(api_key=os.getenv('GROQ_API_KEY')); print('API OK')"

# List audio devices
python -c "import sounddevice; print(sounddevice.query_devices())"

# Check storage
python app.py --stats

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Pressing F9 (or configured hotkey) does nothing
  • No recording indicator appears
  • Application seems unresponsive to hotkey

docs.title: Diagnostic Flowchart
docs.text:
Is the application running?
├─ NO → Start with: python app.py
└─ YES → Is there output in the console when pressing hotkey?
    ├─ NO → Backend issue (see solutions below)
    └─ YES → Check if "Recording" state is shown
        ├─ NO → Hotkey detected but not triggering recording
        └─ YES → Recording works, check paste issue instead

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Check backend configuration:

# Linux X11
HOTKEY_BACKEND=x11

# Linux Wayland
HOTKEY_BACKEND=pynput

# macOS
HOTKEY_BACKEND=keyboard

# Windows
HOTKEY_BACKEND=keyboard

2. Check for hotkey conflicts:

  • Linux: Check desktop environment shortcuts
  • macOS: System Preferences → Keyboard → Shortcuts
  • Windows: Check other applications using F9

3. Change hotkey:

HOTKEY=f10
# or
HOTKEY=ctrl+shift+d

4. Check permissions:

  • Linux: No special permissions needed for X11
  • macOS: Grant Accessibility permission (System Preferences → Security & Privacy → Privacy → Accessibility)
  • Windows: May need to run as administrator

5. Try alternative backend:

HOTKEY_BACKEND=pynput

6. Verify hotkey format:

Valid formats: f1-f12, ctrl+shift+d, alt+f9, cmd+space

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Transcription appears in console
  • Text doesn't appear in target application
  • Clipboard may or may not contain text

docs.title: Animations are choppy or laggy
docs.text:
Symptoms: Floating window animations stutter or drop frames

Diagnostic: 

# Check CPU usage while recording
top  # Linux/macOS
# or Task Manager on Windows

Solutions:

  1. Enable reduced motion: 

    FLOATING_REDUCED_MOTION=1
    This keeps essential animations but removes decorative effects.

  2. Disable animations completely: 

    FLOATING_ANIMATIONS=0

  3. Switch to classic UI: 

    FLOATING_UI_STYLE=classic

  4. Check CPU usage:

  5. Close other resource-intensive applications
  6. Check background processes

  7. Verify system isn't thermal throttling

  8. Reduce window size: 

    FLOATING_WIDTH=150
FLOATING_HEIGHT=40

Note: Animations target 60 FPS and should use <5% CPU on modern hardware.

docs.title: Glassmorphism effect not visible
docs.text:
Symptoms: Floating window appears solid, not translucent

Diagnostic: 

# Check if compositor is running (Linux)
ps aux | grep -i compos

# Check Qt transparency support
python -c "from PySide6.QtWidgets import QApplication; app = QApplication([]); print('Qt OK')"

Solutions:

  1. Ensure glassmorphism is enabled: 

    FLOATING_GLASSMORPHISM=1

  2. Linux: Enable compositor

  3. GNOME/KDE: Usually enabled by default
  4. i3/bspwm: Install and run picom or compton

  5. Check compositor: ps aux | grep picom

  6. Try different accent colors: 

    FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=purple
# or
FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=green

  7. Verify Qt supports transparency:

  8. Update PySide6: pip install --upgrade PySide6

  9. Check Qt version: python -c "from PySide6 import __version__; print(__version__)"

  10. Switch to classic UI if transparency not supported: 

    FLOATING_UI_STYLE=classic

Platform-specific notes: - Linux X11: Requires compositor (picom, compton, etc.) - Linux Wayland: Compositor built-in, should work out of the box - macOS: Works natively, no additional setup - Windows 10/11: Works natively with DWM

docs.title: Accent color not changing
docs.text:
Symptoms: Floating window stays same color despite changing setting

Diagnostic: 

# Check current setting
grep FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR .env

Solutions:

  1. Restart DictaPilot after changing .env:
  2. Stop the application (Ctrl+C)
  3. Edit .env file

  4. Start again: python app.py

  5. Verify spelling: 

    FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=blue     # ✓ Correct
FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=purple   # ✓ Correct
FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=green    # ✓ Correct
FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=Blue     # ✗ Wrong (case-sensitive)
FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=red      # ✗ Not supported

  6. Check for typos in .env file:

  7. No extra spaces: FLOATING_ACCENT_COLOR=blue (not = blue)

  8. No quotes needed: blue (not "blue")

  9. Clear cache and restart: 

    rm -rf __pycache__
python app.py

  10. Verify modern UI is enabled: 

    FLOATING_UI_STYLE=modern

docs.title: Floating window too small or too large
docs.text:
Symptoms: Window size doesn't match expectations

Solutions:

  1. Adjust dimensions: 

    FLOATING_WIDTH=200
FLOATING_HEIGHT=50

  2. Check DPI scaling:

  3. High DPI displays may scale differently

  4. Window should scale appropriately automatically

  5. Try different layout: 

    FLOATING_LAYOUT=pill      # Default, balanced
FLOATING_LAYOUT=circular  # Compact
FLOATING_LAYOUT=card      # Larger

docs.title: Hover effects not working
docs.text:
Symptoms: No visual change when hovering over floating window

Solutions:

  1. Ensure animations are enabled: 

    FLOATING_ANIMATIONS=1
FLOATING_REDUCED_MOTION=0

  2. Check if modern UI is active: 

    FLOATING_UI_STYLE=modern

  3. Restart application after changing settings

docs.title: Performance impact from modern UI
docs.text:
Symptoms: System feels slower with modern UI enabled

Diagnostic: 

# Monitor CPU usage
top -p $(pgrep -f "python.*app.py")

Solutions:

  1. Use reduced motion: 

    FLOATING_REDUCED_MOTION=1
    Reduces animation complexity while keeping functionality.

  2. Disable specific features: 

    FLOATING_GLASSMORPHISM=0  # Disable glass effect
FLOATING_ANIMATIONS=0     # Disable animations

  3. Switch to classic UI: 

    FLOATING_UI_STYLE=classic
    Classic UI has minimal overhead.

  4. Reduce bar count: 

    FLOATING_BAR_COUNT=3  # Fewer bars = less rendering

Expected performance: - CPU usage: <5% during animations - Memory: ~50KB additional for animation state - No impact on transcription quality or speed

docs.title: Diagnostic Flowchart
docs.text:
Is text being transcribed?
├─ NO → See "Transcription Issues" section
└─ YES → Is text in clipboard? (Try Ctrl+V manually)
    ├─ NO → Clipboard not being set (backend issue)
    └─ YES → Paste mechanism failing
        ├─ Try different PASTE_BACKEND
        └─ Check target app accepts programmatic input

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Try different paste backend:

# Linux
PASTE_BACKEND=xdotool  # Requires: sudo apt install xdotool
# or
PASTE_BACKEND=x11
# or
PASTE_BACKEND=pynput

# macOS
PASTE_BACKEND=osascript  # Recommended
# or
PASTE_BACKEND=keyboard

# Windows
PASTE_BACKEND=keyboard  # Recommended
# or
PASTE_BACKEND=pynput

2. Check permissions:

  • Linux: No special permissions for xdotool/x11
  • macOS: Accessibility permission required
  • Windows: May need admin for some apps

3. Verify target app accepts input:

Test with simple apps first: - Linux: gedit, Kate - macOS: TextEdit, Notes - Windows: Notepad

Some apps block programmatic input (security software, password fields).

4. Check focus:

  • Click in text field before releasing hotkey
  • Ensure window is active and focused
  • Some apps require explicit focus

5. Try manual paste:

If text is in clipboard but not pasting: - Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on macOS) manually - If this works, it's a paste backend issue

6. Use delta paste mode:

PASTE_MODE=delta  # Only sends changed characters

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• "Invalid API key" error
  • "Authentication failed" message
  • 401 Unauthorized errors

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Verify API key is set:

# Check .env file exists
cat .env

# Should contain:
GROQ_API_KEY=gsk_...

2. Get valid API key:

  • Visit console.groq.com
  • Sign up or log in
  • Create new API key
  • Copy entire key (starts with gsk_)

3. Check for whitespace:

# Remove any spaces or newlines
GROQ_API_KEY=gsk_your_key_here

4. Test API key:

python -c "from groq import Groq; import os; from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv(); client = Groq(api_key=os.getenv('GROQ_API_KEY')); print('API key valid')"

5. Check internet connection:

ping console.groq.com

6. Check for rate limits:

  • Free tier has usage limits
  • Wait a few minutes and try again
  • Check Groq console for quota

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• "No audio detected" message
  • Recording indicator shows but no transcription
  • Empty or silent audio file

docs.title: Diagnostic Flowchart
docs.text:
Can you record audio with other apps?
├─ NO → System audio issue (check OS settings)
└─ YES → Is microphone listed in device query?
    ├─ NO → Microphone not detected by Python
    └─ YES → Is correct device selected?
        ├─ NO → Set AUDIO_DEVICE
        └─ YES → Check permissions

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. List available devices:

python -c "import sounddevice; print(sounddevice.query_devices())"

2. Select specific device:

# In .env
AUDIO_DEVICE=1  # Use index from device list

3. Check microphone permissions:

  • Linux: Usually no permission needed
  • macOS: System Preferences → Security & Privacy → Privacy → Microphone
  • Windows: Settings → Privacy → Microphone

4. Test microphone:

# Linux
arecord -d 5 test.wav && aplay test.wav

# macOS
python -c "import sounddevice as sd; import soundfile as sf; rec = sd.rec(int(5 * 16000), samplerate=16000, channels=1); sd.wait(); sf.write('test.wav', rec, 16000)"
afplay test.wav

# Windows
# Use Sound Recorder app to test

5. Adjust sample rate:

SAMPLE_RATE=44100  # Try: 16000, 44100, 48000

6. Check system audio settings:

  • Verify correct microphone is selected as default
  • Check input level is not muted
  • Adjust input volume

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Many incorrect words
  • Missing words
  • Garbled transcription

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Improve audio quality:

  • Use external microphone (better than built-in)
  • Reduce background noise
  • Speak clearly at normal pace
  • Position microphone 6-12 inches from mouth

2. Adjust sample rate:

SAMPLE_RATE=44100  # Higher quality

3. Use accurate mode:

DICTATION_MODE=accurate

4. Try different model:

GROQ_WHISPER_MODEL=whisper-large-v3  # More accurate but slower

5. Check internet speed:

  • Slow connection affects transcription
  • Test with: speedtest-cli

6. Reduce cleanup aggressiveness:

CLEANUP_LEVEL=basic
CLEANUP_STRICTNESS=conservative

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Long delay between speaking and paste
  • High CPU/memory usage
  • Application feels sluggish

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Use faster model:

GROQ_WHISPER_MODEL=whisper-large-v3-turbo

2. Disable instant refine:

INSTANT_REFINE=0

3. Use heuristic mode:

SMART_MODE=heuristic  # No LLM calls

4. Use delta paste:

PASTE_MODE=delta

5. Lower sample rate:

SAMPLE_RATE=16000

6. Reduce cleanup:

CLEANUP_LEVEL=basic

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
• Application exits unexpectedly
  • Python error messages
  • Segmentation fault

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text:
1. Check Python version:

python --version
# Should be 3.10 or higher

2. Reinstall dependencies:

pip install --force-reinstall -r requirements.txt

3. Check for conflicts:

# Use virtual environment
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # Linux/macOS
venv\Scripts\activate     # Windows
pip install -r requirements.txt

4. Check logs:

# Run with verbose output
python app.py 2>&1 | tee dictapilot.log

5. Update system libraries:

  • Linux: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade
  • macOS: brew update && brew upgrade
  • Windows: Update via Windows Update

docs.title: Linux
docs.text:
Wayland hotkey issues: 

HOTKEY_BACKEND=pynput

X11 paste fails: 

sudo apt install xdotool
PASTE_BACKEND=xdotool

Permission denied: 

# Don't run as root
# Check file permissions
chmod +x app.py

docs.title: macOS
docs.text:

docs.title: Voice Commands Reference
docs.text: DictaPilot3 supports smart voice commands for editing and controlling your dictation. Commands are processed in real-time and work across all applications.

docs.title: Command Categories
docs.text:
• Editing Commands - Delete, undo, clear text
  • Replacement Commands - Replace words or phrases
  • Formatting Commands - Capitalize, rewrite, fix grammar
  • Control Commands - Ignore, skip utterances
  • Inline Corrections - Self-correct while speaking

docs.title: Delete Last Segment
docs.text:
Remove the most recently dictated text.

Commands: - "delete that" - "delete previous" - "undo" - "undo that" - "scratch that" - "remove that" - "remove previous" - "take that out" - "erase that" - "drop that" - "backspace that"

Example: 

You say: "Hello world"
Text: "Hello world"

You say: "delete that"
Text: "" (cleared)

Example with continuation: 

You say: "The quick brown fox"
Text: "The quick brown fox"

You say: "delete that jumps over the lazy dog"
Text: "jumps over the lazy dog"

docs.title: Clear All Text
docs.text:
Remove all dictated text and start fresh.

Commands: - "clear all" - "clear everything" - "reset" - "reset all" - "start over" - "wipe all" - "wipe everything" - "erase all"

Example: 

You say: "This is a long paragraph with multiple sentences"
Text: "This is a long paragraph with multiple sentences"

You say: "clear all"
Text: "" (all cleared)

docs.title: Clear (Simple)
docs.text:
Short form of clear all.

Commands: - "clear" - "reset" - "wipe"

Example: 

You say: "Some text here"
Text: "Some text here"

You say: "clear"
Text: "" (cleared)

docs.title: Replace Word or Phrase
docs.text:
Replace specific text with new text.

Commands: - "replace [target] with [replacement]" - "change [target] to [replacement]" - "swap [target] for [replacement]"

Example: 

You say: "Hello world"
Text: "Hello world"

You say: "replace hello with goodbye"
Text: "goodbye world"

Example with phrases: 

You say: "The quick brown fox jumps"
Text: "The quick brown fox jumps"

You say: "replace quick brown with slow red"
Text: "The slow red fox jumps"

docs.title: Rewrite with Tone
docs.text:
Rewrite text in a specific tone or style.

Commands: - "rewrite [tone]" - "rephrase [tone]" - "polish [tone]" - "improve [tone]" - "make it [tone]"

Supported tones: - formal - polite - casual - friendly - concise - professional - natural

Example: 

You say: "Hey can you help me out"
Text: "Hey can you help me out"

You say: "rewrite formal"
Text: "Could you please assist me"

Example: 

You say: "I need this done ASAP"
Text: "I need this done ASAP"

You say: "make it polite"
Text: "I would appreciate if this could be completed soon"

docs.title: Capitalize
docs.text:
Capitalize the last word or phrase.

Commands: - "capitalize that" - "capitalize last" - "make that capitalized" - "uppercase that"

Example: 

You say: "python is great"
Text: "python is great"

You say: "capitalize that"
Text: "Python is great"

docs.title: Fix Grammar
docs.text:
Correct grammar in the text.

Commands: - "fix grammar" - "correct grammar" - "fix that"

Example: 

You say: "I is going to the store"
Text: "I is going to the store"

You say: "fix grammar"
Text: "I am going to the store"

docs.title: Ignore Utterance
docs.text:
Don't include the current utterance in the transcript.

Commands: - "ignore" - "ignore that" - "ignore this" - "ignore it" - "skip" - "skip that" - "disregard" - "omit" - "don't include" - "don't include that" - "do not include" - "don't add" - "do not add" - "leave that out" - "cancel that" - "nevermind" - "never mind"

Example: 

You say: "This is important"
Text: "This is important"

You say: "ignore this is not important"
Text: "This is important" (second utterance ignored)

Trailing ignore: 

You say: "Maybe we should add this ignore that"
Text: "Maybe we should add this" (trailing part ignored)

docs.title: Self-Correction While Speaking
docs.text:
Correct yourself mid-sentence using natural phrases.

Correction phrases: - "no" - "no no" - "nope" - "sorry" - "I mean" - "actually"

Example: 

You say: "Use Python no JavaScript for this project"
Text: "Use JavaScript for this project"

Example: 

You say: "The meeting is at 3 PM sorry I mean 4 PM"
Text: "The meeting is at 4 PM"

docs.title: Negation Replacement
docs.text:
Use "not X but Y" pattern for corrections.

Pattern: - "not [wrong] [right]" - "not [wrong] use [right]" - "not [wrong] but [right]"

Example: 

You say: "not Python JavaScript"
Text: "JavaScript"

Example: 

You say: "We'll use not MySQL but PostgreSQL"
Text: "We'll use PostgreSQL"

docs.title: Smart Cleanup Features
docs.text: DictaPilot automatically cleans up your dictation:

docs.title: Filler Word Removal
docs.text:
Automatically removes: "uh", "um", "erm", "ah", "hmm"

Example: 

You say: "Um this is uh a test"
Text: "This is a test"

docs.title: Filler Phrase Removal
docs.text:
Removes: "you know", "I mean", "kind of", "sort of"

Example: 

You say: "This is kind of important you know"
Text: "This is important"

docs.title: Repeated Word Removal
docs.text:
Removes duplicate words.

Example: 

You say: "The the quick brown brown fox"
Text: "The quick brown fox"

docs.title: Punctuation Cleanup
docs.text:
Fixes repeated punctuation.

Example: 

You say: "What??? Really!!!"
Text: "What? Really!"

docs.title: Command Prefaces
docs.text:
You can preface commands with natural phrases:

  • "oh no"
  • "oops"
  • "please"
  • "hey"
  • "okay"
  • "wait"
  • "well"

Example: 

You say: "Hello world"
Text: "Hello world"

You say: "oh no delete that"
Text: "" (deleted)

docs.title: Tips for Best Results
docs.text:
1. Speak naturally - Don't over-enunciate or speak robotically
  2. Use pauses - Brief pauses help separate commands from content
  3. Be specific - "replace hello with hi" is clearer than "change it"
  4. Combine commands - "delete that and start over" works
  5. Practice common commands - Muscle memory helps with frequently used commands

docs.title: Cleanup Levels
docs.text:
Control how aggressively DictaPilot cleans up your speech:

# In .env file
CLEANUP_LEVEL=basic      # Minimal cleanup
CLEANUP_LEVEL=balanced   # Moderate cleanup (default)
CLEANUP_LEVEL=aggressive # Maximum cleanup

docs.title: Smart Edit Modes
docs.text:
Choose between heuristic and LLM-based editing:

SMART_MODE=heuristic  # Fast, rule-based
SMART_MODE=llm        # AI-powered (default)

docs.title: Dictation Modes
docs.text:
Balance speed vs accuracy:

DICTATION_MODE=speed    # Fast, minimal processing
DICTATION_MODE=balanced # Moderate (default)
DICTATION_MODE=accurate # Maximum quality

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Commands not recognized? - Speak clearly and at normal pace - Check that SMART_EDIT=1 in .env - Try SMART_MODE=llm for better recognition

Cleanup too aggressive? - Set CLEANUP_LEVEL=basic - Set CLEANUP_STRICTNESS=conservative

Want more control? - Disable smart editing: SMART_EDIT=0 - Use manual editing after dictation

docs.title: smart_editor.py
docs.text: Smart text editing with voice commands and cleanup.

docs.title: Classes
docs.text:
TranscriptState 

@dataclass
class TranscriptState:
    """Maintains dictation state."""

    segments: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    output_text: str = ""
    last_action: str = "append"
    context: Optional[DictationContext] = None

docs.title: Functions
docs.text:
smart_update_state(state, utterance, context=None) 

def smart_update_state(
    state: TranscriptState,
    utterance: str,
    context: Optional[DictationContext] = None
) -> Tuple[TranscriptState, str]:
    """
    Update transcript state with new utterance.
    Handles voice commands and text cleanup.

    Args:
        state: Current transcript state
        utterance: New spoken text
        context: Dictation context (app, tone, language)

    Returns:
        Tuple of (updated_state, action_taken)
        action_taken: "append", "undo", "clear", "ignore"

    Example:
        >>> state = TranscriptState()
        >>> state, action = smart_update_state(state, "Hello world")
        >>> print(state.output_text)
        "Hello world"
        >>> state, action = smart_update_state(state, "delete that")
        >>> print(state.output_text)
        ""
    """

llm_refine(text, context=None) 

def llm_refine(
    text: str,
    context: Optional[DictationContext] = None
) -> str:
    """
    Refine text using LLM (Groq Chat API).

    Args:
        text: Raw transcribed text
        context: Dictation context for tone/language

    Returns:
        Refined text with grammar fixes and cleanup

    Example:
        >>> text = "um this is uh a test"
        >>> refined = llm_refine(text)
        >>> print(refined)
        "This is a test."
    """

is_transform_command(utterance) 

def is_transform_command(utterance: str) -> bool:
    """
    Check if utterance is a transformation command.

    Args:
        utterance: Spoken text

    Returns:
        True if utterance is a command (rewrite, fix grammar, etc.)

    Example:
        >>> is_transform_command("rewrite formal")
        True
        >>> is_transform_command("hello world")
        False
    """

docs.title: paste_utils.py
docs.text: Text pasting with delta paste support.

docs.title: Functions
docs.text:
paste_text(text, old_text="", mode="delta", backend="auto") 

def paste_text(
    text: str,
    old_text: str = "",
    mode: str = "delta",
    backend: str = "auto"
) -> bool:
    """
    Paste text to active application.

    Args:
        text: Text to paste
        old_text: Previous text (for delta paste)
        mode: "delta" or "full"
        backend: Paste backend to use

    Returns:
        True if paste succeeded, False otherwise

    Example:
        >>> paste_text("Hello world")
        True
        >>> paste_text("Hello there world", old_text="Hello world", mode="delta")
        True  # Only pastes "there "
    """

compute_delta(old_text, new_text) 

def compute_delta(old_text: str, new_text: str) -> Tuple[int, str]:
    """
    Compute minimal edit to transform old_text to new_text.

    Args:
        old_text: Previous text
        new_text: New text

    Returns:
        Tuple of (num_backspaces, text_to_add)

    Example:
        >>> compute_delta("Hello world", "Hello there world")
        (5, "there world")  # Delete "world", add "there world"
    """

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: All configuration can be set via environment variables. See Configuration Guide for complete list.

docs.title: Core Settings
docs.text:
GROQ_API_KEY: str          # Required: Groq API key
HOTKEY: str = "f9"         # Global hotkey
SMART_EDIT: bool = True    # Enable smart editing

docs.text:
SAMPLE_RATE: int = 16000   # Recording sample rate
CHANNELS: int = 1          # Number of channels

AUDIO_DEVICE: int = None   # Audio device index

docs.title: Backend Settings
docs.text:
HOTKEY_BACKEND: str = "auto"  # "auto", "keyboard", "pynput", "x11"
PASTE_BACKEND: str = "auto"   # "auto", "keyboard", "pynput", "xdotool", "x11", "osascript"

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
All public functions may raise:

  • ValueError: Invalid arguments
  • RuntimeError: Runtime errors (device not available, etc.)
  • APIError: API call failures (network, authentication, etc.)

Example error handling:

try:
    audio = record_audio(duration=5)
    text = transcribe_audio(audio)
    paste_text(text)
except ValueError as e:
    print(f"Invalid input: {e}")
except RuntimeError as e:
    print(f"Runtime error: {e}")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Unexpected error: {e}")

docs.text:
# Add custom paste backend
def paste_custom(text: str) -> bool:
    """Custom paste implementation."""
    # Your implementation
    return True

# Register in paste_utils.py
PASTE_BACKENDS["custom"] = paste_custom

docs.title: Custom Command
docs.text:
# Add custom voice command
import re
from smart_editor import TranscriptState

CUSTOM_CMD = re.compile(r"^my command (.+)$", re.IGNORECASE)

def handle_custom_command(state: TranscriptState, utterance: str) -> str:
    match = CUSTOM_CMD.match(utterance)
    if match:
        arg = match.group(1)
        # Your logic here
        return f"Processed: {arg}"
    return utterance

docs.title: DictaPilot3 Architecture
docs.text: Technical overview of DictaPilot3's system design and component interactions.

docs.title: 1. Application Core (app.py)
docs.text:
Responsibilities: - Application lifecycle management - Component initialization and coordination - Event loop and threading - GUI management (floating window, tray icon) - Configuration loading

Key Classes: - GUIManager - Manages floating status window - HotkeyManager - Handles global hotkey registration - RecordingState - Tracks recording state machine

Threading Model: - Main thread: GUI event loop (Qt) - Hotkey thread: Global hotkey monitoring - Recording thread: Audio capture - Transcription thread: API calls - Worker threads: Background processing

docs.title: 2. Audio Recording (recorder.py)
docs.text:
Responsibilities: - Audio capture from microphone - Real-time level monitoring - Silence trimming - Buffer management

Key Functions: 

def record_audio(duration=None, callback=None):
    """
    Record audio from microphone

    Args:
        duration: Max recording duration (None = until stopped)
        callback: Function called with audio level updates

    Returns:
        numpy array of audio samples
    """

Audio Pipeline: 1. Initialize PortAudio stream 2. Capture audio chunks (configurable chunk size) 3. Calculate RMS level for visualization 4. Buffer audio data 5. Trim silence from start/end 6. Return audio as numpy array

Configuration: - SAMPLE_RATE: Recording sample rate (default: 16000 Hz) - CHANNELS: Number of audio channels (default: 1 - mono) - TRIM_SILENCE: Enable silence trimming (default: True) - SILENCE_THRESHOLD: Threshold for silence detection (default: 0.02)

docs.title: 3. Transcription (transcriber.py)
docs.text:
Responsibilities: - Send audio to transcription service - Handle API communication - Retry logic and error handling - Health checking

Key Functions: 

def transcribe_audio(audio_data, sample_rate=16000):
    """
    Transcribe audio using Groq Whisper API

    Args:
        audio_data: numpy array of audio samples
        sample_rate: Sample rate of audio

    Returns:
        Transcribed text string
    """

Transcription Flow: 1. Convert numpy array to WAV format 2. Send to Groq Whisper API 3. Parse JSON response 4. Extract transcribed text 5. Handle errors and retries

Backends: - Groq API (default): Cloud-based Whisper - Local whisper.cpp: Offline transcription (requires setup)

Models: - whisper-large-v3-turbo: Fast, good accuracy (default) - whisper-large-v3: Slower, best accuracy - whisper-medium: Balanced - whisper-small: Fastest, lower accuracy

docs.title: 4. Smart Editor (smart_editor.py)
docs.text:
Responsibilities: - Voice command detection and execution - Text cleanup (filler words, repeated words) - Inline correction handling - LLM-based refinement - State management

Architecture:

Raw Transcript
      │
      ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ Command         │
│ Detection       │
└────┬────────────┘
     │
     ├─ Command? ──► Execute Command ──► Updated State
     │
     └─ Content ──► Cleanup Pipeline ──► Cleaned Text
                          │
                          ├─ Heuristic Mode (fast)
                          │   ├─ Remove filler words
                          │   ├─ Dedupe repeated words
                          │   ├─ Fix punctuation
                          │   └─ Inline corrections
                          │
                          └─ LLM Mode (accurate)
                              └─ Send to Groq Chat API
                                  └─ Grammar, tone, cleanup

Key Classes: 

@dataclass
class TranscriptState:
    """Maintains dictation state"""
    segments: List[str]           # Individual utterances
    output_text: str              # Current full text
    last_action: str              # Last action taken
    context: DictationContext     # App context

Command Patterns: - Deletion: _UNDO_RE, _CLEAR_RE - Replacement: _REPLACE_RE - Formatting: _REWRITE_RE, _GRAMMAR_RE - Control: _IGNORE_RE - Inline: _INLINE_CORRECTION_RE

Cleanup Pipeline: 1. Preface removal: Remove "oh no", "oops", etc. 2. Filler word removal: Remove "um", "uh", "hmm" 3. Repeated word deduplication: "the the" → "the" 4. Inline corrections: "no, I mean X" → "X" 5. Punctuation cleanup: Fix repeated punctuation 6. Capitalization: Sentence-initial caps

LLM Integration: 

def llm_refine(text: str, context: DictationContext) -> str:
    """
    Refine text using LLM

    Args:
        text: Raw transcribed text
        context: Dictation context (app, tone, language)

    Returns:
        Refined text
    """

docs.title: 5. Paste Utility (paste_utils.py)
docs.text:
Responsibilities: - Text pasting to target application - Delta paste computation - Multiple backend support - Clipboard management

Delta Paste Algorithm: 

def compute_delta(old_text: str, new_text: str) -> Tuple[int, str]:
    """
    Compute minimal edit to transform old_text to new_text

    Returns:
        (num_backspaces, text_to_add)

    Example:
        old: "Hello world"
        new: "Hello there world"
        returns: (5, "there world")  # Delete "world", add "there world"
    """

Paste Backends:

Backend Platform Method Speed Reliability x11 Linux X11 Direct X11 API Fast High xdotool Linux xdotool command Medium High osascript macOS AppleScript Medium High keyboard All Keyboard simulation Fast Medium pynput All Python keyboard lib Slow High

Backend Selection Logic: 1. Check PASTE_BACKEND env var 2. If auto, detect platform and display server 3. Select optimal backend for platform 4. Fall back to pynput if preferred fails

docs.title: Complete Dictation Flow
docs.text:
1. User presses F9
   └─► HotkeyManager detects press
       └─► RecordingState → RECORDING
           └─► GUIManager updates UI (show "Recording")

2. Audio capture begins
   └─► recorder.py starts PortAudio stream
       └─► Audio chunks buffered
           └─► RMS levels sent to GUI for visualization

3. User releases F9
   └─► HotkeyManager detects release
       └─► RecordingState → PROCESSING
           └─► GUIManager updates UI (show "Processing")
               └─► Audio recording stops

4. Transcription
   └─► Audio sent to transcriber.py
       └─► Groq Whisper API call
           └─► JSON response parsed
               └─► Raw transcript extracted

5. Smart editing
   └─► Raw transcript → smart_editor.py
       ├─► Command detection
       │   ├─► If command: execute and update state
       │   └─► If content: continue to cleanup
       └─► Cleanup pipeline
           ├─► Heuristic mode: regex-based cleanup
           └─► LLM mode: Groq Chat API refinement

6. Paste
   └─► Cleaned text → paste_utils.py
       ├─► Delta paste computation
       │   └─► Calculate minimal edit
       └─► Backend execution
           ├─► Set clipboard
           └─► Simulate Ctrl+V (or direct paste)

7. Complete
   └─► RecordingState → IDLE
       └─► GUIManager updates UI (show "Done" briefly)
           └─► Transcription stored in history

docs.title: Thread Safety
docs.text:
Main Thread: - Qt event loop - GUI updates - User interaction

Hotkey Thread: - Global hotkey monitoring - Signals main thread on press/release

Recording Thread: - Audio capture - Level monitoring - Signals main thread with audio data

Transcription Thread: - API calls (blocking I/O) - Signals main thread with results

Worker Pool: - LLM refinement (optional) - Background tasks

Synchronization: - threading.Lock for shared state - queue.Queue for inter-thread communication - Qt signals/slots for GUI updates

docs.title: State Machine
docs.text:
IDLE ──► RECORDING ──► PROCESSING ──► IDLE
  ▲                                     │
  └─────────────────────────────────────┘

State Transitions: - IDLE → RECORDING: Hotkey pressed - RECORDING → PROCESSING: Hotkey released - PROCESSING → IDLE: Transcription complete

docs.title: Optimization Strategies
docs.text:
Audio Processing: - Use appropriate sample rate (16kHz for speech) - Minimize buffer size for lower latency - Trim silence to reduce API payload

Transcription: - Use turbo model for speed - Batch multiple short recordings - Cache common phrases

Smart Editing: - Heuristic mode for speed (no LLM) - Lazy LLM refinement (background) - Regex compilation and caching

Paste: - Delta paste reduces keystrokes - Direct API calls faster than subprocess - Clipboard caching

docs.title: Memory Management
docs.text:
• Audio buffers released after transcription
  • Transcription history limited (configurable)
  • LRU cache for LLM responses
  • Profile lazy loading

docs.title: Error Categories
docs.text:
Recoverable: - API rate limits → Retry with backoff - Network timeouts → Retry - Audio device busy → Wait and retry

User-fixable: - Invalid API key → Show error, prompt for key - Missing permissions → Show instructions - Hotkey conflict → Suggest alternative

Fatal: - Missing dependencies → Exit with message - Unsupported platform → Exit with message - Configuration corruption → Reset to defaults

docs.title: Python Style Guide
docs.text:
Follow PEP 8 with these specifics:

Formatting: - 4 spaces for indentation (no tabs) - Max line length: 120 characters - Use double quotes for strings - Use f-strings for formatting

Naming: - snake_case for functions and variables - PascalCase for classes - UPPER_CASE for constants - Private functions/methods: _leading_underscore

Example:

# Good
def transcribe_audio(audio_data: np.ndarray, sample_rate: int = 16000) -> str:
    """Transcribe audio using Groq API."""
    if audio_data is None:
        raise ValueError("audio_data cannot be None")

    client = get_groq_client()
    result = client.audio.transcriptions.create(
        file=audio_file,
        model=GROQ_WHISPER_MODEL
    )
    return result.text

# Bad
def TranscribeAudio(audioData, sampleRate=16000):
    if audioData == None:
        raise ValueError("audioData cannot be None")
    client=get_groq_client()
    result=client.audio.transcriptions.create(file=audio_file,model=GROQ_WHISPER_MODEL)
    return result.text

docs.text:

from typing import Optional, List, Tuple

def process_text(

docs.text:
Use Google-style docstrings:

def transcribe_audio(audio_data: np.ndarray, sample_rate: int = 16000) -> str:
    """
    Transcribe audio using Groq Whisper API.

    Args:
        audio_data: numpy array of audio samples
        sample_rate: Sample rate of audio (default: 16000)

    Returns:
        Transcribed text string

    Raises:
        APIError: If API call fails
        ValueError: If audio_data is invalid

    Example:
        >>> audio = record_audio(duration=5)
        >>> text = transcribe_audio(audio)
        >>> print(text)
        "Hello world"
    """

docs.title: Writing Tests
docs.text:
Use pytest for all tests:

# tests/test_smart_editor.py
import pytest
from smart_editor import TranscriptState, smart_update_state

def test_delete_command():
    """Test delete that command."""
    state = TranscriptState()
    state, _ = smart_update_state(state, "Hello world")
    assert state.output_text == "Hello world"

    state, action = smart_update_state(state, "delete that")
    assert state.output_text == ""
    assert action == "undo"

def test_replace_command():
    """Test replace command."""
    state = TranscriptState()
    state, _ = smart_update_state(state, "Hello world")
    state, _ = smart_update_state(state, "replace hello with goodbye")
    assert "goodbye" in state.output_text.lower()

@pytest.mark.parametrize("command,expected_action", [
    ("delete that", "undo"),
    ("clear all", "clear"),
    ("ignore", "ignore"),
])
def test_commands(command, expected_action):
    """Test various commands."""
    state = TranscriptState()
    state, _ = smart_update_state(state, "Some text")
    state, action = smart_update_state(state, command)
    assert action == expected_action

def test_full_dictation_flow():
    # Record → Transcribe → Edit → Paste
    audio = record_audio(duration=2)
    text = transcribe_audio(audio)
    state = TranscriptState()
    state, _ = smart_update_state(state, text)
    success = paste_text(state.output_text)
    assert success

from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock

@patch('transcriber.Groq')
def test_transcribe_with_mock(mock_groq):
    mock_client = MagicMock()
    mock_client.audio.transcriptions.create.return_value.text = "test"
    mock_groq.return_value = mock_client

    result = transcribe_audio(np.zeros(16000))
    assert result == "test"

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text:

  • Python 3.10 or higher
  • Microphone access
  • Administrator privileges (for some features)

docs.title: 1. Install Python
docs.text:
Download Python from python.org: - Check "Add Python to PATH" during installation - Install for all users (recommended)

Verify installation: 

python --version

docs.title: 2. Clone Repository

Using Git: 

git clone https://github.com/RehanSajid136602/DictaPilot.git
cd DictaPilot

Or download ZIP from GitHub and extract.

docs.title: 3. Run Setup Script
docs.text:
setup\setup_windows.bat

docs.text:
Create .env file in project root: 

echo GROQ_API_KEY=your_api_key_here > .env

Or use Notepad: 

notepad .env
Add: GROQ_API_KEY=your_api_key_here

docs.text:

HOTKEY_BACKEND=keyboard
PASTE_BACKEND=keyboard

docs.text:
keyboard (Recommended):

pynput (Alternative):

pynput (Alternative):

  5. Right-click shortcut → Properties
  6. Target: C:\Users\YourName\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python312\python.exe "C:\path\to\DictaPilot\app.py"

  7. Start in: C:\path\to\DictaPilot

  8. Move to Startup:

  9. Press Win+R
  10. Type shell:startup
  11. Move shortcut to this folder

docs.title: Custom Hotkey Combinations
docs.text:
# Modifier keys
HOTKEY=ctrl+shift+d
HOTKEY=alt+f9
HOTKEY=win+space

docs.title: Multiple Audio Devices
docs.text:
# List all devices
python -c "import sounddevice; print(sounddevice.query_devices())"

# Select by index
AUDIO_DEVICE=2

# Or by name
AUDIO_DEVICE="USB Microphone"

docs.title: Network Configuration
docs.text:
# Use proxy
set HTTP_PROXY=http://proxy.example.com:8080
set HTTPS_PROXY=http://proxy.example.com:8080

docs.title: Firewall Configuration
docs.text:
If Windows Firewall blocks network access:

  1. Windows Defender Firewall → Allow an app
  2. Click "Change settings"
  3. Click "Allow another app"
  4. Browse to python.exe
  5. Add and enable for Private and Public networks

docs.title: Windows 10
docs.text:
• Works with all features
  • May need admin for some apps
  • Microphone permission in Settings → Privacy

docs.title: Windows 11
docs.text:
• Enhanced security features
  • Microphone permission in Settings → Privacy & security
  • May show additional security prompts
  • Recommended to use latest Python version

docs.title: DLL Load Failed
docs.text:
Problem: ImportError: DLL load failed

Solution: Install Visual C++ Redistributable: - Download from Microsoft website - Install both x86 and x64 versions

docs.title: PortAudio Error
docs.text:
Problem: Error loading PortAudio

Solution: 

pip uninstall sounddevice soundfile
pip install sounddevice soundfile

docs.title: Encoding Errors
docs.text:
Problem: UnicodeDecodeError or encoding issues

Solution: 

# In .env
PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8
